seoTitle: Port Broughton vs Port Davis — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Port Broughton and Port Davis. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Port Broughton vs Port Davis: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Port Broughton and Port Davis.
heading: Port Broughton vs Port Davis — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Port Broughton, South Australia and Port Davis, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Port Broughton has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Port Davis (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Port Broughton or Port Davis safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Port Broughton has a flood risk score of 30/100, while Port Davis scores 54/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Port Broughton scores 26/100 for bushfire risk. Port Davis scores 26/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Port Broughton or Port Davis?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Port Broughton has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.6% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
